title: A-stack external-control covariance on loose pulse-quality tiers

traditional.method: grouped-run-heldout Ridge using B pair features plus event-matched A controls
traditional.metric: A-any subset heldout sigma68 residual width ns

ml.method: grouped-run-heldout bounded ExtraTrees using B pair features plus event-matched A controls
ml.metric: A-any subset heldout sigma68 residual width ns

finding: Looser raw pulse-quality tiers increase A/B coincidence statistics, but A-stack controls still do not provide a statistically secure held-out reduction of B-stack pair residual width.

leakage.interpretation: A controls lose event matching but preserve run marginals
positive leakage sentinel; should be unrealistically small
A controls lose event matching but preserve run marginals
positive leakage sentinel; should be unrealistically small

next_tickets: S05d: repeat the loose-tier A-control test with sorted ROOT quality variables such as hrdMaxTS and trap summaries to test whether sorted pulse-shape cuts isolate better A/B coincidences.
S05e: build a run-level A/B coincidence-rate model across current and target settings to separate beam-rate effects from detector-local B covariance.
